insights.insight: Ad tech charges $5–$150 CPA for probabilistic attribution; this protocol settles verified purchase events at roughly $0.10 each — a 50–200x cost delta that isn't a pricing negotiation, it's a different category of evidence.

With Chrome completing cookie deprecation and fingerprinting under regulatory pressure, the industry has no cross-ecosystem, identity-free purchase signal at scale — verified receipts are the only deterministic alternative that doesn't require a user login or a data broker relationship.

Amazon knows Amazon spend; Kroger knows Kroger spend; neither knows what their customers buy elsewhere — a protocol that captures cross-retailer purchase data without identity creates an information advantage no single first-party dataset can replicate.

The network's geographic footprint is building through non-metro markets across 15+ states — populations that are systematically undertargeted by digital advertising and underrepresented in first-party data programs, meaning the signal covers a segment most brand datasets miss.

The 14-day brand mix spans grocery (Kroger, H-E-B, King Soopers), mass retail (Walmart, Target), warehouse (Costco), and home improvement (Home Depot) — verified cross-category purchase data that no single retail media network can produce.

Token velocity has been range-bound for six consecutive measurement periods despite geographic expansion, meaning intake is steady but not yet compounding; brands evaluating this as a scaled data source should treat current volume as early-stage signal, not mature inventory.
